## Plan Summary

**Title**: Migrate to Qdrant + SQLite, Add OpenAI Provider

**Overview**: Replace the Postgres+pgvector+tsvector stack with Qdrant Cloud (dense + sparse vectors, server-side RRF via Query API) for retrieval, and SQLite+aiosqlite for chat/metadata persistence. Add OpenAI as a first-class provider alongside OpenRouter for both chat completions and embeddings, configurable independently. The route layer, ingest pipelines, SSE framing, citation handling, refusal detection, and per-document collapse behavior all stay externally identical — the swap is contained behind `rag/vector_store.py`, `db/sqlite.py`, and a new `llm/providers.py` abstraction.

**Type**: REFACTOR + NEW_CAPABILITY
**Complexity**: HIGH
**Estimated Tasks**: 29

---

## NOT Building (Scope Limits)

**CRITICAL FOR REVIEWERS**: These items are **intentionally excluded** from scope. Do NOT flag them as bugs or missing features.

- **No new auth layer.** `DEFAULT_USER_ID = "default-user"` stays. Vestigial auth tables (`users`, `user_messages`, `signup_attempts`) are dropped from the schema entirely.
- **No SQLAlchemy ORM at runtime.** Alembic uses SQLAlchemy under the hood (sync engine for migrations) but `repository.py` stays raw SQL via `aiosqlite`. Same contract as before: all SQL in one file.
- **No automatic Postgres → SQLite/Qdrant migration at runtime.** Existing Postgres installs use the one-shot `scripts/migrate_pg_to_qdrant.py` CLI script offline. The app's startup lifespan does not detect or attempt to read from Postgres.
- **No multi-tenant collection support in Qdrant.** Single collection (`firstspirit_docs`) with `source_type` as a payload filter — mirrors today's single-tenant model.
- **No FastEmbed-based dense embeddings.** Dense vectors continue to come from OpenAI/OpenRouter (`text-embedding-3-small`, 1536-dim). FastEmbed is used ONLY for the BM25 sparse vector path.
- **No prompt-cache support on OpenAI native provider.** OpenAI's auto-prompt-cache works without `cache_control` blocks (transparent). Anthropic-specific `cache_control` blocks are stripped when `LLM_PROVIDER=openai`. If OpenAI introduces an explicit cache primitive later, that's a follow-up.
- **No retrieval cache.** The metadata cache in `retriever_hybrid._document_cache` was a workaround for Postgres `get_document` overhead; Qdrant payloads now carry the metadata so this cache is unnecessary and is removed.
- **No CI workflow.** Tests run locally only (matching current state — no `.github/workflows/`).
- **No frontend changes.** Frontend has no DB or LLM provider awareness; it consumes `/api/*` only.
- **No reranker, no re-embedding strategy change.** Same chunker (Docling HybridChunker, cl100k_base, 512 tokens), same dense model, same RRF k=60.

## Validation Commands

### Level 1: STATIC_ANALYSIS

```bash
cd app/backend && uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check . && uv run mypy .
```
EXPECT: Exit 0, no errors or warnings.

### Level 2: UNIT_TESTS

```bash
cd app/backend && uv run pytest tests/ -xvs
```
EXPECT: All tests pass. Test count goes from 86 to ~95+ (added vector_store, llm_providers, repository_sqlite, retriever_hybrid tests).

### Level 3: FRONTEND

```bash
cd app/frontend && bun install && bun run tsc --noEmit && bun x biome check src && bun run test
```
EXPECT: All pass — frontend should be untouched.

### Level 4: INTEGRATION (manual, with real Qdrant Cloud)

```bash
# Set deploy/.env with real QDRANT_URL, QDRANT_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY
docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml up --build -d
docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml ps
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/sources/sync -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"kind":"url_list"}'
```
EXPECT: App boots, ingest writes to both SQLite and Qdrant, retrieval returns hits with valid citation markers, SSE stream is byte-compatible with the old behavior.

### Level 5: PROVIDER SMOKE

Run Level 4 twice — once with `LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=openrouter`, once with `LLM_PROVIDER=openai EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=openai CHAT_MODEL=gpt-4o EMBEDDING_MODEL=text-embedding-3-small`. Both must produce streaming responses with citations.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml up --build` brings up a single `app` container (no postgres). The container reaches healthy state.
- [ ] `POST /api/sources/sync {"kind":"url_list"}` ingests the seed URL list. Chunks land in Qdrant; document rows land in SQLite.
- [ ] `POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages` streams an answer with `[c:<id>]` citation markers, an `event: sources` payload, and a `data: [DONE]` terminator. Frontend renders source chips and deep-link anchors identically to before.
- [ ] All 4 LLM tools (`search_documents`, `keyword_search_documents`, `semantic_search_documents`, `get_document`) work end-to-end against Qdrant + SQLite.
- [ ] Refusal phrase "the documentation library does not cover that topic" still triggers citation suppression in `_is_refusal`.
- [ ] Per-document chunk collapse still works (one chip per document, not one per chunk).
- [ ] `LLM_PROVIDER=openai` produces a working chat stream against `https://api.openai.com/v1`; switching back to `openrouter` works without code changes.
- [ ] `EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=openai` produces working ingest embeddings against OpenAI native.
- [ ] No `asyncpg`, no `pgvector`, no `tsvector`, no `:vector` casts in the codebase (`rg -n "asyncpg|pgvector|tsvector|::vector|search_vector|to_tsvector" app/backend/` returns zero matches).
- [ ] `scripts/migrate_pg_to_qdrant.py --help` runs and produces sensible output for an existing Postgres user.
- [ ] All 4 levels of validation commands pass with exit 0.

---

## Patterns to Mirror

| Pattern | Source File | Lines | Purpose |
|---------|-------------|-------|---------|
| Module-level `init_*` / `close_*` / `_acquire` provider shape | `app/backend/db/postgres.py` | 1-65 | `db/sqlite.py` mirrors this surface |
| Repository function signature (`async with _acquire() as conn`) | `app/backend/db/repository.py` | 108-111 | Keep every public function's signature; swap internals to aiosqlite |
| RuntimeError wrapping for external services (log + wrap, no silent fallback) | `app/backend/rag/embeddings.py` | 62-70 | Apply to Qdrant client errors |
| Hybrid retriever public surface (`retrieve_hybrid()`, `invalidate_cache()`) | `app/backend/rag/retriever_hybrid.py` | 1-186 | Callers stay unchanged after rewrite |
| SSE streaming contract (`data:`, `event: sources`, `[DONE]`) | `app/backend/routes/messages.py` | 1-330 | Byte-identical post-migration |
| `stream_chat` generator with tool-call loop / heartbeat / error handling | `app/backend/llm/openrouter.py` | 1-422 | Provider-factory refactor target |
| Alembic initial schema layout (table set, indices) | `app/backend/alembic/versions/0001_initial.py` | 1-260 | New SQLite migration mirrors minus pgvector/tsvector/auth |
| Env-var reads centralised in `config.py` | `app/backend/config.py` | 1-142 | New Qdrant/OpenAI vars added here only |

## Mandatory Reading (P0 - Implementation Agent MUST Read)

- `app/backend/db/repository.py` (1-779) — every SQL string + signature to port
- `app/backend/rag/retriever_hybrid.py` (1-186) — exact RRF semantics + hit shape
- `app/backend/rag/tools.py` (1-569) — 4 tool executors call into retriever + repository
- `app/backend/routes/messages.py` (1-330) — SSE streaming contract must be byte-identical
- `app/backend/llm/openrouter.py` (1-422) — full `stream_chat` generator (refactor target)
- `app/backend/alembic/versions/0001_initial.py` (1-260) — source-of-truth schema
- `app/backend/config.py` (1-142) — all env reads here, nowhere else

---

## Key Risks (from plan)

- BM25 keyword quality vs Postgres tsvector on proper nouns/acronyms — run A/B eval on 10-20 known queries; tune to `Qdrant/bm42` if quality lower.
- FastEmbed model download (~30MB ONNX) on first call — pre-warm in `ensure_collection()`; persist via `/app/data/fastembed-cache`.
- SQLite `WHERE x IN (?,?,?)` placeholder generation — generate from `len(values)`, NEVER interpolate values themselves.
- aiosqlite autocommit semantics — every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE in `repository.py` MUST end with `await conn.commit()`. Plan recommends an auto-commit context manager in `_acquire()`.

---

## Migration Path (for existing Postgres users)

`scripts/migrate_pg_to_qdrant.py` — one-shot CLI, idempotent:
- Reads chunks from a Postgres DSN (asyncpg one-off, NOT via repository)
- Upserts dense vectors + payloads to Qdrant
- Re-derives BM25 sparse vectors via FastEmbed locally

README will document the one-shot migration flow.
